content.full_text:
California
-Fair Political
Practices Commission
November 4,
1993
chris Micheli
T&x
& Corporate Counsel
California Manufacturers Association
1121 L Street, Suite 900
Sacramento,
CA
95814
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our File No.
A-93—388
Dear Mr. Micheli:
You have requested confirmation of telephone advice on behalf
of the California Manufacturers Association political action
committee
(“cMA PAC”)
concerning the lobbying and personal use
provisions of the Political Reform Act
(the “Act”).
1
!
QUESTIONS
.
1.
May you serve as legal counsel to the
CMA PAC if you are
a lobbyist?
2.
May
CMA PAC pay your California State Bar dues?
CONCLUSIONS
1.
The Act does not prohibit or restrict you from serving
as legal counsel to
CMA PAC.
2.
CMA PAC may not pay your membership dues to the
California State Bar.
FACTS
You are employed as a lobbyist and attorney for the
California Manufacturers Association.
You have been asked to
1/
Government Code Sections
81000—91015.
All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at
2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000,
q.
All references to regulations are to Title
2,
Division
6 of the California Code of Regulations.
428 J Street, Suite 800
•
P.O. Box 807 •
Sacramento, CA
95804-0807
•
(916) 322-5660


A—93—388
‘Page 2
serve as legal counsel to the Association’s sponsored political
action committee.
In a telephone conversation with me on October
25,
1993, you stated that you do not have authority to approve any
expenditure from the political action committee.
You must pay
annual dues to the State Bar of California in order to maintain
active membership.
ANALYSIS
Section 86205 prohibits lobbyists from engaging in certain
lobbying-related activities.
In addition, lobbyists are
prohibited from making or arranging a gift aggregating more than
ten dollars in a calendar month to a reportable official.
(Section 86203.)
However, there is nothing in the Act that would
restrict a lobbyist from serving as legal counsel to a political
action committee.
You have been asked to provide professional services to
CMA
PAC.
Section 89513(b) provides that cainpaign funds cannot be used
for professional services unless such services are directly
related to a political, legislative, or governmental purpose.
Because the direct beneficiary of a membership dues payment to the
California State Bar would be yourself rather than
CMA PAC,
such a
payment would not satisfy the standard in Section 89l3(b).
If you have questions concerning this letter, please contact
meat
(916)
322—5662.
Sincerely,
Steven G.
Churchwell
‘1 Counse
Lynda Cassad.
Assistant Division Chief
Technical Assistance Division
